title: Species Classification
wikitext:
[]: Species Classification?             Quote Post by KingXanaduu » Tue Oct 04, 2011 3:21 pm As Eva fans, we are all aware of the differences between the angels and humans in terms of their relation to each other. That the angels are described as “humans who cast aside human form”. They are similar to humans, but have simply taken on a different path of power instead of knowledge. Both humans and angels are considered to be similar species, despite their vast differences. Now, this had me thinking on a similar concept involving the humans in the Evaverse. Compared to us, would the humans in the Evaverse be considered a different species to us as well? For example, while the humans in the Evaverse are similar to us in both form, knowledge, and emotions, there are vast differences between us and them. In our universe, we do not possess A.T. Fields and although we do have souls, they are not even close to being as tangible as the souls involved in Meta-physical Biology as presented in the series. The people in the Evaverse operate on a similar, yet very different realm of physics compared to ours. So I ask: Because of these differences, should the humans in the Evaverse be classified as a different, yet similar species compared to us, such as how we, Homo Sapiens, are similar to the now extinct species, Homo Neanderthal? Should we classify them as "Homo Lilin Alternus"? What is your opinion on this thought?

[]: Re: Species Classification?             Quote Post by Sachi » Tue Oct 04, 2011 3:24 pm xanderkh wrote: and although we do have souls

[]: evas have souls             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Tue Oct 04, 2011 4:02 pm ^ agree until we can deploy A.T. fields, I'm fine with us being called Homo sapiens.

[]: Re: evas have souls             Quote Post by Stryker » Tue Oct 04, 2011 4:04 pm bobbyfischer's ghost wrote: until we can deploy A.T. fields, I'm fine with us being called Homo sapiens.

[]:             Quote Post by SaVaGe » Tue Oct 04, 2011 4:56 pm Of course we have AT Feilds! If we didn't, we'd be tang!

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Tue Oct 04, 2011 5:21 pm SaVaGe wrote: Of course we have AT Feilds! If we didn't, we'd be tang!

[]:             Quote Post by SaVaGe » Tue Oct 04, 2011 6:17 pm Stryker wrote: Yes, but we can't deploy them...

[]: Re: Species Classification?             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Tue Oct 04, 2011 8:15 pm Sachi wrote: Objection.

[]: Re: Species Classification?             Quote Post by KingXanaduu » Tue Oct 04, 2011 8:23 pm TehDonutKing wrote: Also, please correct me if I'm wrong, but isn't Homo sapien neanderthal a race, rather than a species?

[]: Re: Species Classification?             Quote Post by ReiAyanami25 » Tue Oct 04, 2011 9:34 pm Sachi wrote: Objection.

[]: Re: Species Classification?             Quote Post by KingXanaduu » Tue Oct 04, 2011 10:03 pm ReiAyanami25 wrote: Also, the origin of the Angels is somewhat unclear. Did they come from earth like humans, or did they come from another place in the universe? this can also lead to the debate of the origin of humans in Evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Tue Oct 04, 2011 10:59 pm The Classified Information is from a noncanonical PS2 game that Anno may or may not have had come up with. Considering his attitude in interviews, giving contradictory answers and telling people to find their own meaning. That, and Gainax remains coy on the exact details of Anno's involvement.

[]: Still what it is. Bl             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Wed Oct 05, 2011 12:37 am TehDonutKing wrote: The Classified Information is from a noncanonical PS2 game that may or may not have had come up with.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Oct 05, 2011 2:54 pm The conceit of Evangelion is that everything descended from the First Ancestral Race and is capable of thinking thoughts is "human." The differences between Angels and Lilim is a matter of Fruits, not genetics. And yea, we're the same thing as Lilim since there doesn't seem to be any considerable differences (can you prove we don't have AT Fields?)

[]:             Quote Post by arkiel » Wed Oct 05, 2011 3:12 pm Sure we gots a body-wide system what helps us maintain our form. Its called DNA. If you want to deploy it, just cut an artery. There isn't much to recommend Eva humans being different from us. Scientific understanding just branched off in a weird direction after the intercession of a huge bit of alien biology.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Wed Oct 05, 2011 4:09 pm I'm pretty sure the proposal isn't canon either.

[]: Still the explanation. Bl             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Wed Oct 05, 2011 4:18 pm ^ It's roughly 3rd tier, but that doesn't matter. The point is that these ideas have existed for as long as Eva has, they did not just come about when the game did. Not to mention, much of EoE hints at it, and indeed, wouldn't make much sense without the FARs being the explanation they're getting at. EDIT: Also, this interview: SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Wed Oct 05, 2011 4:30 pm arkiel wrote: Sure we gots a body-wide system what helps us maintain our form. Its called DNA. If you want to deploy it, just cut an artery.

[]:             Quote Post by KingXanaduu » Wed Oct 05, 2011 6:51 pm bobbyfischer's ghost wrote: As opposed to humans in eva who liquify when Lilith deploys her A.T. field.

[]:             Quote Post by arkiel » Wed Oct 05, 2011 10:12 pm All of which could be salient components of a larger, subtle hollistic energy field. http://integrallife.com/apply/science-technology/sheldrakes-theory-morphogenesis If you're looking for any kind of definitive evidence that humans and Eva humans are distinct, I don't think its gonna happen any time soon.
